question_text: A flat-bottomed boat has a weight of 5000 N and its hull (the bottom) is in contact with water over an area of 50 m². What pressure does the boat exert on the water?
explanation: Pressure is calculated using the formula P = F/A, where F is the force (weight of the boat) and A is the area of contact. P = 5000 N ÷ 50 m² = 100 Pa. This relatively low pressure is why boats can float without sinking – the pressure is spread over a large area.
